## Hotfix: agent registration silently failed on systems with `systemd-xdg-autostart-generator`

On distros where systemd's user manager wraps `/etc/xdg/autostart/*.desktop`
into `app-*@autostart.service` units (Arch's default, Fedora, openSUSE),
`sentinel-polkit-agent` was being launched as a child of `user@.service`
instead of the compositor. Its kernel `sessionid` then matched the manager
session, not the graphical session — so polkit's `RegisterAuthenticationAgent`
rejected it with:

```
org.freedesktop.PolicyKit1.Error.Failed: Passed session and the session
the caller is in differs. They must be equal for now.
```

Sentinel kept running but never became the registered agent. On COSMIC
that meant cosmic-osd's password dialog took over; on Sway / Hyprland /
plain wlroots it meant **no agent at all**.

## Fix

`packaging/xdg-autostart/sentinel-polkit-agent.desktop` now sets
`X-GNOME-Autostart-enabled=false`. systemd-xdg-autostart-generator
honours that key and skips the entry. The compositor's own autostart
mechanism (cosmic-session, Hyprland exec-once, etc.) continues to spawn
Sentinel directly, so it inherits the graphical sessionid and registers
cleanly.

## Action required

Reinstall the v0.4.1 package, then **log out and back in**. XDG autostart
entries are only re-evaluated at session start.

```bash
yay -Syu sentinel              # AUR
sudo apt install ./sentinel_0.4.1-1_amd64.deb
sudo dnf install ./sentinel-0.4.1-1.x86_64.rpm
```

After login:

```bash
pgrep -af sentinel-polkit-agent
journalctl --user -t sentinel-polkit-agent --since "5 minutes ago" | grep -i register
```

You should see `registered as polkit auth agent` and no
`Passed session and the session the caller is in differs` error.

## Known limitation: cosmic-osd coexistence

On cosmic-comp, cosmic-osd registers as polkit agent on session start,
beating Sentinel to the slot. polkit only allows one agent per session,
so Sentinel registration fails with `An authentication agent already
exists`. cosmic-osd has no flag to opt out of the polkit half (issue
filed at <https://github.com/pop-os/cosmic-osd/issues/197>).

Workaround until cosmic-osd ships such a flag:

```bash
sudo chmod -x /usr/bin/cosmic-osd
```

cosmic-session won't be able to spawn it, Sentinel takes the polkit
slot. You lose cosmic-osd's keyboard / volume / brightness OSDs in the
process — accept the tradeoff per your preference, or stay on
cosmic-osd's password prompt for now.

## No other changes

This is a packaging-only fix. No code changes in `pam-sentinel`,
`sentinel-helper`, or `sentinel-polkit-agent` since v0.4.0.
